reviews.review_title: The value of ig on vr
reviews.review_description:
In today’s digital world, social media continues to evolve in new and exciting ways. One example is Instagram on virtual reality (VR). While some people criticize it and say it is unnecessary or distracting, Instagram on VR actually offers many positive features. It creates a more immersive experience, encourages creativity, and allows people to connect in new ways that traditional apps cannot.

First, Instagram on VR provides a more immersive experience than using it on a phone. Instead of simply scrolling through pictures on a screen, users can feel like they are inside the content. For example, they can view photos and videos in a 3D space or explore virtual environments shared by others. This makes the app more engaging and enjoyable. Even though some people think VR is too complicated, it can make social media feel more real and interactive.

Another benefit of Instagram on VR is that it boosts creativity. Users are not limited to just posting photos or short videos; they can design virtual spaces, create 3D art, and share unique experiences. This opens the door for new kinds of content that are impossible on regular devices. While critics may say it is too different or hard to use, it actually gives people more freedom to express themselves in creative ways.

In addition, Instagram on VR helps people connect with others on a deeper level. Instead of just liking or commenting on posts, users can interact with each other in shared virtual spaces. They can attend virtual events, hang out with friends, or meet new people from around the world. This can make social interactions feel more personal and meaningful. Although some people worry about too much screen time, VR can make online communication feel more like real-life interaction.

In conclusion, Instagram on VR may not be accepted by everyone, but it has many advantages. It creates immersive experiences, encourages creativity, and improves the way people connect online. Like any technology, it has its downsides, but it also has great potential. Instead of dismissing it, people should consider how it can positively change the future of social media.

reviews.review_title: Not only can I not get rid of it...
reviews.review_description: ...but it's not even the 3D version — I'm forced to have some age-old piece of historical programming on my headset. It makes me sadder that Meta have a constantly processing thread in the OS that always checks if you've removed these apps and instantly reinstalls them — how self-conscious/centred do you have to be to not let someone have the choice to remove something they might never even use.
